text:
Once upon a time, there was a cheerful little bird who lived in a forest full of goldenrod. She was brightest orange you could imagine. Every morning she sang a cheerful song about flying further than anyone else.

One very unusual morning, she sang so high that she vanished into a cotton candy cloudscape.

She flew further than ever before, seeing endless cotton candy-colored skies. Birds sang dulcet dulcets there. She flew over fields of cotton candy wildflowers.

Eventually, she flew too high for clouds. She plummeted downwards! Downwards she went!

She whacked into a cotton candy mint farm. The mint was lovely, but she was too heavy.

She danced about happily, minty.

The reindeer liked minty things.

They chased her... for days. She flew faster, singing. Birds sang back.

At last, she flew over the tallest mountain she'd ever seen. She landed on top.

They never chased her again. Just soft moss.

She became
